name: Free Application for Federal Student Aid (FAFSA)
description: The FAFSA is the gateway application for all federal student aid, including Pell Grants, Direct Loans, and Work-Study. Most states and colleges also use FAFSA data to award their own aid.
eligibility: U.S. citizen or eligible non-citizen with a valid Social Security number; enrolled or accepted for enrollment in an eligible degree/certificate program; demonstrates financial need (for most programs); maintains satisfactory academic progress.
award_amount: Not an award itself — determines eligibility for federal, state, and institutional aid programs.
application_process: Complete online at studentaid.gov/fafsa. Requires FSA ID for student and one parent (dependent students). Provide prior-prior year tax data (auto-imported via IRS DRT when available). List up to 20 colleges to receive ISIR.
timeline: Opens October 1 each year. Priority deadline varies by state and college — many are February 1–March 1. Must be renewed annually.
advisor_notes: File as early as possible after October 1. Some state grants are first-come, first-served. Even families who believe they won't qualify should file — many schools require FAFSA for merit aid consideration.
